page: Microsoft Azure EC64ads_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECadsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

The server includes 2458 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and there are no onboard GPUs.

Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is classified as memory-optimized. The high memory density combined with substantial local storage capacity makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and enterprise applications. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities, though the lack of hardware acceleration limits its utility for GPU-dependent tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC64ads_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECadsv6 family, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high-density 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the instance is provisioned with 2458 GB of local disk space and supports Premium Storage configurations. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is engineered for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, and large-scale data processing tasks that require significant memory capacity and dedicated compute resources without the need for graphics acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC64ads_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, designed for large databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, half a terabyte of system memory, and substantial local storage capacity.
